ops.title: PageIndex-style outline summaries for ccx web measured and rejected; hybrid search wins; 4 product gaps found
ops.body:
2026-07-08 spike (branch `spike/webnav`, memo `docs/webnav-spike.md` — NOT on main): tested PageIndex-style outline enrichment for [[ccx-web-op-family]] with 180 isolated haiku trials (6 real docs × 6 gold questions × 5 arms). Verdict: **rejected**. Shipped hybrid `web search` was the most accurate AND cheapest arm (92%, $0.029/trial); extractive digests scored below plain headings on structured docs (88% vs 96%); sonnet per-node summaries only tied plain headings at +14% trial cost plus 1.2M tokens of indexing. No `web annotate` op, no plugin indexer agent.

Four measured product gaps worth fixing instead (each a real failure observed during the spike, none needs summaries):

1. Raw `.md` URLs are destroyed — `Extract` HTML-escapes markdown bodies into a single structureless section (`internal/web/extract.go:26#jd0m`).
2. Keyless PDFs ingest as `%PDF` binary "markdown" via the http tier (`internal/web/fetch.go:46#jkmh`); jina 401s keyless.
3. `web read --section` rejects a document's printed section numbers (outline shows dual numbering `§10.6.6 [5.6.7.]`) with a bare "section not found" — should resolve or suggest (`internal/web/web.go:150#pd0c`).
4. Degenerate single-section outlines make section reads head-of-page only; search is the only path to deep content.

Bench harness reusable pattern: spike/webnav/run.py — spawnllm headless trials need `output_format="stream-json" + verbose` (schema forces `--output-format json` which hides tool events AND there's no on-disk transcript with `--no-session-persistence`); bench/.venv must be Python 3.13 (3.14 breaks on spacy wheels).
